location.office_time_required: In-person environment: Union Square office designed for ambitious builders

level_signals: Direct engagement with CIOs, managing partners, general counsels at largest law firms
Owns strategy, roadmap, and execution independently
Background at 'high-growth B2B SaaS companies' (plural, experienced)

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: An experienced, senior Product Manager with a strong background building products at high-growth B2B SaaS companies.
Direct experience owning enterprise features and functionality, such as data retention, audit logging, permissions, RBAC, ethical walls, APIs, or admin tooling, at another high-growth company.
A track record of partnering with enterprise buyers and administrators, and comfort engaging senior stakeholders like CIOs, CISOs, and legal or compliance leaders.
Strong product judgment and the ability to balance the rigor that security and governance demand with the speed and ambition of a fast-moving startup.
Excellent collaboration skills, with a history of partnering tightly with design, engineering, security, and go-to-market functions to ship products.
High standards, low ego, and a bias toward ownership and outcomes.
A genuine interest in solving the new, unsolved problems of governing and operating AI-native software at enterprise scale.

scope.surface: Enterprise surface area: data retention, audit logging, roles and permissions, ethical walls, metrics and ROI tracking, external-facing APIs, and admin controls
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, AI-native legal platform, enterprise-focused
primary_metrics_inferred: ROI tracking accuracy and enterprise scrutiny resistance
cross_functional_partners: CIOs / managing partners / general counsels (customer stakeholders)
core_responsibilities: Own the strategy, roadmap, and execution for the Enterprise surface area: data retention, audit logging, roles and permissions, ethical walls, metrics and ROI tracking, external-facing APIs, and the broader set of admin controls our customers depend on.
Rethink how governance works in an AI-native world, defining how a platform built around AI agents can be controlled, audited, and trusted by the most security-conscious organizations.
Build AI-native experiences for admin users, reimagining how administrators configure, monitor, and reason about their deployment rather than replicating legacy admin consoles.
Partner directly with CIOs, managing partners, and general counsels at some of the largest law firms and enterprises in the world to understand their requirements and turn them into product.
Work closely with design and engineering to ship secure, polished, high-quality capabilities with a strong bar for craft and reliability.
Help customers measure and articulate the value of Legora through metrics and ROI tracking that stand up to enterprise scrutiny.
Collaborate with sales, security, and the wider org to unblock enterprise deals and ensure a coherent experience as the platform grows.

green_flags: Company has scaled to $100M+ ARR with 1,000+ customers across 50+ countries — strong signal of product-market fit
Clear product ownership area with specific, enumerable capabilities (not vague mandate)
High-visibility role with direct engagement with C-suite enterprise buyers
Genuinely novel problem space (AI-native governance) rather than incremental feature work
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail (medical, dental, vision, 401K match, unlimited PTO, parental leave)
red_flags: In-person (Union Square, NYC) with no mention of hybrid or remote flexibility — fully onsite requirement
No minimum years of experience stated, making seniority bar ambiguous
No equity or stock compensation mentioned despite being a growth-stage startup
